records.ety: [Cf. Icel. hvirfilvindr, Sw. hvirfvelvind, Dan. hvirvelvind, G. wirbelwind. See Whirl, and Wind, n.]

defs: A violent windstorm of limited extent, as the tornado, characterized by an inward spiral motion of the air with an upward current in the center; a vortex of air. It usually has a rapid progressive motion.
quotes: The swift dark whirlwind that uproots the woods. And drowns the villages.
notes: ☞ Some meteorologists apply the word whirlwind to the larger rotary storm also, such as cyclones.

defs: Fig.: A body of objects sweeping violently onward.
